entry.title: Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud - Blog 3
entry.content:
Introduction 

Dia duit 👋and welcome to the third instalment of this five-part blog series.
 
Previously, in blog 2 we discussed the following: 

  • Crafting compelling stories and visual masterpieces 

  • Embrace the future with Optimized Design Experience (ODE) 

 

In this blog, I am going to share with you the steps we took to create a live connection between Datasphere and SAC. Creating a live connection between Datasphere and SAC is crucial for improving data analysis capabilities and support better decision-making. Having the live connection between the two platforms allows for real-time data analysis meaning users can access the most up-to-date information for their analysis, leading to more accurate insights.

 

These steps include:

 

  1. Add Datasphere and SAC as ‘Trusted Origins’ with each other

  2. Create the live connection in SAC

  3. Create a SAC story & test your connection

 

Add Datasphere and SAC as ‘Trusted Origins’ with each other

This step has been discussed in depth under the ‘Elevating Your Data Connection Experience’ in blog 1 of this blog series. Just to give a quick overview, trusted origins is security-based concept that establishes a trusted relationship between the two platforms. It allows secure communication and data exchange, ensuring that requests and responses between Datasphere and SAC are authenticated and authorized.

 
 

Create the Live Connection

By creating a live connection between Datasphere and SAC, users can build stories directly against the data that is modelled in Datasphere. Analytical Datasets must be exposed for consumption first in Datasphere. If you've established a live connection but can't find the data you need when creating your story, ensure that it's deployed and configured for use within your Datasphere system.

 

In SAC, go to connections an create a new connection. Expand the ‘Connect to Live Data’ and select SAP Datasphere.

 

 

 

When you select SAP Datasphere, you’ll you asked to enter the Datasphere Host and HTTPS Port. You’ll also be asked to give your live connection a name. Where to find the Host & HTTPS Port?
 
 

 

 

Your Datasphere host name can be found from your browser’s address bar when logged on to your Datasphere tenant. The HTTPS port is always 443.
 

 

 

SAML Single Sign On is preselected as the authentication method allowed. When you click on OK, you'll be prompted to log on to Datasphere. Once you enter valid username and password the connection will be successfully set up.
 

 

 

Create a SAC story & test that your connection is successful

Once the connection is successfully created, the users can directly access and analyze the data from Datasphere without the need to duplicate modelling efforts or create a separate model within SAC. To learn more about this step, refer to blog 2 of this blog series under the section titled 'Crafting Compelling Stories and Visual Masterpieces'.
 

 

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, establishing a live connection between Datasphere and SAC is pivotal for enhancing data analysis capabilities and facilitating better decision-making. By following the outlined steps, including adding both platforms as trusted origins, creating the live connection in SAC, and testing its success, users can seamlessly access and analyze real-time data from Datasphere within SAC.

entry.title: Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud - Blog 4
entry.content:
Introduction 

Hello and welcome to the fourth instalment of this five-part series. Previously we discussed: 

  • Crafting compelling stories and visual masterpieces
  • Embrace the future with Optimized Design Experience (ODE)

 

At the moment, there are 2 ways to integrate Datasphere with SAC for Planning Purposes:

  1. Sending data from Datasphere to SAC via OData Service
  2. Sending data from SAC to Datasphere via CDI Connection (Cloud Data Integration)

 

In this blog, I want to provide the steps we followed to create an OData Service Connection to send data from Datasphere to SAC. By integrating Datasphere Actuals into SAC, we lay the foundation for strategic decision-making, driving business growth and agility.


Sending Data from Datasphere to SAC via OData Service

 

 

Our goal for this task is to consume actuals from Datasphere into SAC for reporting and planning purposes. We will combine the actuals from Datasphere with planning data in SAC.

Here is a list of steps we took to import Datasphere actual data into SAC:

1. Identify the OData Source (Data Service URL) in Datasphere

In order for us the identify the Data Service URL we went to the following link. Within this URL, you can see all the assets available within the spaces available in your Datasphere tenant. 

 

Within this URL, we copied the assetRelationDataURL for the dataset we required. This is your Data Service URL. We then looked up our chosen assetRelationDataURL using the URL in the figure below. This will get us the meta data from the OData Service.

NOTE: To view the data from your chosen asset, add the dataset name again at the end of the URL.

2. Lookup your redirect URI in SAC

The redirect URI in SAC is the specific endpoint URL. The URI is the destination to which the authentication server redirects the user's browser after authentication has been successfully completed.

We added an OData Services connection in our SAC tenant. We then change the Authentication Type to OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code. At the bottom of this window, we can see the Redirect URI.

 

3. Create the OAuth Client in Datasphere

We now want to create an OAuth client in Datasphere. We need the Redirect URI from the above step to create the OAuth client. We do this by going to System >> Administration >> App Integration and click on the ‘Add a New OAuth Client’. Once you paste the Redirect URI and click Add, you’ll get a list of the following information which you will need to take a note of.

  • OAuth Client ID
  • Secret
  • Authorization URL
  • Token URL

 

4. Create OData Connection in SAC

Within SAC, we go to Connections >> Add a Connection and chose OData Service. You need to now add the following connection parameters:

  • Data Service URL = assetRelationalDataUrl
  • OAuth Client ID
  • Secret
  • Token URL 
  • Authorisation URL

5. Import from OData Connection

We now need to create a model in SAC. We chose OData Services as the data source and click the new connection we just made. We then create a new query and select which dimensions and measures you want when building that query.

 

You can see that we have now consumed actuals data from datasphere to SAC! At this stage you can clean data and fix any issues. You have successfully imported the actuals data from OData Connection!!

 

6. Create Story and Test

As you can see here, we created a story. We added a table to our story. We chose the query we created above as the data source for the table. We added ‘Category’ as a column and using the version management functionality we can choose both the Actual and Plan category. This shows Actual data from Datasphere and Plan data within SAC.

 

Conclusion

 

To recap, we have successfully sent Actual data from Datasphere to SAC via OData Service. To do this we identified the data service URL in Datasphere. Using this and other information we created an OAuth Client. We then completed the cycle by creating the OData Connection in SAC. Once we have successfully created the OData connection, we created a story and tested to see the Actual data from Datasphere in SAC.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.11
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.11 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.407 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP
  • Data Exploration
    • Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer
    • Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer
  • Data Integration
    • Data Export API - Delta Difference
    • Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections
  • Story Design
    • Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides
    • Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations
  • Story Extensibility
    • Retrieving non-input-ready variables as dynamic text or through script API

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP

If you accidentally delete your service instance in the SAP BTP cockpit, it can be restored within seven days. Learn more here.

 

Data Exploration

 

Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer

A "+Add Calculations" button has been added in the Structure section of the Available Objects panel. This allows the creation of calculations using structure members in two structured queries on BW.

 

Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer

The last used values for defined components and interactions are now kept so that when data analyzer is reopened, the options are the same. That way as a data analyzer user you can now set the following:

  • Show/hide the filter line by default
  • Open/close individually the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list 
  • Expand/collapse the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list by default
  • Set the system and connection type by default in the data source browser
  • Keep all settings in the individual export dialogs for PDF, Microsoft Excel and CSV export (except for Export name)

 

Data Integration

 

Data Export API - Delta Difference

Data Export Service API subscriptions has been extended to support the calculation of the difference between old and new numerical delta values when performing a Delta Extraction, rather than just new measure values.

 

Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections

Users can now select Client Certificate authentication and use an X.509 certificate for generic OData connections, SAP Business ByDesign Analytics and SAP S4/HANA Cloud connections.

 

Story Design

 

Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides

The optimized story experience now supports export as Google Slides. Google Slides can be saved as a new presentation, or the slides can be added to an existing presentation in a Google Drive folder.

This is not supported in the lite viewer.

 

Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations

The Lite Viewer now supports color by dimension for the bubble layer in geo maps. Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension will no longer cause an automatic redirect to the Full Viewer.

There are few improvements that have been made to the interactivity of Geographical Visualizations which includes:

  • Improved Interaction with Color by Dimension Bubble
  • Consolidation of Drill with Tooltip
  • Improved Threshold Tooltip

 

Story Extensibility

In the optimized story experience, dynamic text and variable related script APIs such as getVariables and getVariableValues now support non-input-ready variables.

Retrieve the variables that are not input ready, such as customer exit variables, from a live connection to SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW):

  • As Dynamic Text
  • Through script APIs
    • getDataSource().getVariableValues()
    • getDataSource().getVariables

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Dedicated OData Analytics APIs for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM offers a generic OData interface to access analytics of the differents use cases (Projects Management, Tasks Management, Health Monitoring...).

In addition to this generic OData interface, some use cases like "Requirements" provide a specific OData interface which is more convenient to use since it returns a dataset with the real name of dimensions and measures instead of generic names like d1k, d1v.

In SAP Analytics Clouds, you can consume the specific OData interface out-of-the-box by selecting the dedicated entity which is available using the same connection that you'd use to access the generic entity DataSet. Just select entity "Requirements" to access analytics of of SAP Cloud ALM Requirements scenario.

Depending on the scenario, you might have to filter some dimensions. In the case of Requirements all filters are optional which means you can just select all dimensions and measures you need and press "Next" to load all requirement analytics data in your model.

The frame time dimension "period" and "resolution" are still relevant with the specific interface and will impact the number of records returned. 

Once data imported in the SAP Analytics Cloud model you can manipulate the dimensions and measures using their real name as opposed to the generic DataSet interface which exposes generic dimension names (dxk,dxv) and generic measure names (mxk,mxv).

Although the format of generic OData interface and specific OData interface differ, they return the same content. 

References

  • SAP Cloud ALM Analytics API
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Requirement analytics API
  • Access SAP Cloud ALM analytics from SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Access SAP Cloud ALM analytics catalog through OData interface

entry.title: 上新啦！SAP 分析云 2024.11 版功能更新
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能 (BI)、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本文介绍了 SAP 分析云 2024.11 版本的新功能。这些新功能已经在 SAP 分析云 FastTrack 客户的系统上线。对于 SAP 分析云季度发布周期 (QRC) 客户，此版本及其功能将作为 QRC 2024 年第3季度版本的一部分提供。在此处可以查看 QRC 发布时间表。 

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本1.0.407，以利用所有数据采集类型。

代理的最低版本仍为 1.0.75，但以下情况除外：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库的版本 1.0.99
  • SAP universe （UNX） 的版本 1.0.365（SAP 分析云代理需要其他设置步骤，有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP 说明 3262098）
  • SAP Business Warehouse （BW） 的版本 1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View 的版本 1.0.235
  • File Server版本 1.0.248

有关详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

有关SAP BW与SAP分析云集成的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅SAP Note 2541557了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP分析云的 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 实时访问Universe时的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的简易部署工具包。

  • 注意：为避免下载旧版本，以及可能让用户面对Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的不当绑定问题，只有三个最新版本的工具包可供下载。

有关云代理版本变更管理详情，请参阅 SAP Note 3264839。

更新亮点

  • 管理和服务管理
    • 防止丢失通过 BTP 意外删除的 SAP 分析云租户
  • 数据发掘
    • 在数据分析器中为两个结构BW查询添加对结构成员的计算
    • 数据分析器中保留上次使用值
  • 数据集成
    • 数据导出 API – 增量差异
    • 支持基于 OData 连接的 X509 证书身份验证
  • 故事设计
    • 优化故事体验现在支持导出为 Google 幻灯片
    • 在地理可视化对象中，精简版查看器现在支持按维着色的气泡图层
  • 故事可扩展性
    • 以动态文本或脚本 API 的形式检索非输入就绪变量

 

管理和服务管理

防止丢失通过 BTP 意外删除的 SAP 分析云租户

如果您不小心在 SAP BTP 主控室中删除了服务实例，可在七天内恢复。点击此处此处了解更多信息。

 

数据发掘

在数据分析器中为两个结构BW查询添加对结构成员的计算

在“可用对象”面板的“结构”部分中增加了“+添加计算”按钮。这样就可以在 BW的两个结构化查询中使用结构成员创建计算。

 

数据分析器中保留上次使用值

现在，上次使用的已定义部件和交互值将被保留，在重新打开数据分析器时，选项将保持不变。数据分析器用户现在可以设置以下内容：

  • 默认显示/隐藏过滤器行
  • 分别打开/关闭样式面板和构建器面板和可用对象列表
  • 默认情况下，展开/折叠样式设置面板和生成器面板和可用对象列表
  • 默认情况下，在数据源浏览器中设置系统和连接类型
  • 保留 PDF、Microsoft Excel 和 CSV 导出对话框中的所有设置（导出名称除外）

 

数据集成

数据导出 API - 增量差异

数据导出服务 API 订阅已扩展，以支持在执行增量提取时计算新旧数值增量值之间的差异，而不仅仅是新度量值。

 

基于 OData 连接的 X509 证书身份验证

用户现在可以选择客户端证书身份验证，并将 X.509 证书用于通用 OData 连接、SAP Business ByDesign Analytics 和 SAP S4/HANA Cloud 连接。

 

故事设计

优化故事体验现在支持导出为 Google 幻灯片

优化故事体验现在支持导出为 Google 幻灯片。Google 幻灯片可以另存为新演示文稿，也可以添加到 Google Drive 文件夹现有演示文稿中。

精简查看器中不支持这一功能。

 

地理可视化对象中，精简版查看器现在支持按维着色的气泡图层

精简查看器现在支持地理地图中的气泡图层按维着色。按维着色的气泡图层将不再自动重新定向到完整查看器。

“地理可视化对象”的交互性也有一些改进：

对地理可视化的交互性进行了一些改进，其中包括：

  • 改进了与按维着色的气泡的交互
  • 使用工具提示合并钻取
  • 改进阈值工具提示

 

故事可扩展性

在优化故事体验中，动态文本和与变量相关的脚本 API（如 getVariables 和 getVariableValues）现在支持非输入就绪变量。

从与 SAP Business Warehouse （SAP BW） 的实时连接中检索未准备好输入的变量，例如客户出口变量：

  • 作为动态文本
  • 通过脚本 API
    • getDataSource（）.getVariableValues（）
    • getDataSource（）.getVariables

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP 知识库文章。

如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP Analytics Cloud 即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
The objective of this blog post is to help provide an overview to establish the live data connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and the SAP SuccessFactors system.  

With this new enhancement, you can copy the live connection (used in People Analytics Stories) to the Enterprise edition of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Some additional capabilities available to this live connection in SAC Enterprise are:

  • Data consolidation and cross application reporting

  • Use both Acquired and Live connections in a single story.

  • Build custom analytical applications. 

  • Scripting Capabilities

  • Custom Widgets/ Widget Add-ons

  • Composites

  • View stories in presentation mode

  • Theming capabilities

  • Public Models can be re-used.

 

Below is the architecture of the connection options available to an SAP SuccessFactors system.

Pre-requisites: 

  • You must be assigned the Story Admin permission in SAP SuccessFactors.

  • You must be assigned the BI Admin role in SAP Analytics Cloud to be able to copy the live connection to SAP Analytics Cloud.

  • You have enabled and configured Stories in People Analytics on your SAP SuccessFactors tenant.

  • You have a common Identity Authentication service between SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Analytics Cloud (for single sign on).

 
Creating the live connection in the SAP Analytics Cloud: 
  • From the side navigation, choose   Connections -> (Add Connection).  

The Select a data source dialog will appear.  

  • Expand Connect to Live Data and select SAP Cloud Application.  

  • You are now asked to Sign in, From the Select application dropdown pick SAP SuccessFactors.  

For Application URL enter your SAP SuccessFactors URL e.g.: https://xxx.sf.com.

Enter your Company ID, the Sign In button is activated, Click it.

  1. If SSO is enabled, once you select Sign In, you are signed in automatically.

If not, a Log on dialog appears to connect to SAP SuccessFactors. Provide user credentials to Log On.

The validation of your credentials and role can take up to a minute.

  1. You will now see a list of connections available.   Select it and click Next to copy it.

Currently “SAP SuccessFactors Reporting” connection is supported. In the subsequent release we will support “SAP SuccessFactors Learning” connection as well.

 Note: If this connection already exists in the tenant, an error message is displayed. Please delete the old connection and try again.

  1. Select the connection you want to clone and click Create.  

  7. The new connection details are displayed in the Clone Summary page.

Select Finish to complete the workflow.

Note: After copying the SuccessFactors connection, you must refresh the page.  

Tip: It is recommended that you use the same IAS (Identity Authentication Service) for both SuccessFactors and SAP Analytics Cloud for Single sign on else you will always be prompted for your credentials in all workflows.

Conclusion:  You have configured a successful live connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and the SAP SuccessFactors system.    

 

Using the Modeler

To create the first model, select Modeler from the side panel

Select “Live Data Model” option.

  • Select “SAP Cloud Application” in the System Type dropdown. Select “SAPSFSFREP” as the connection.

  • The credential for the connection is validated. Once confirmed the OK button is enabled.

  • The modeler provides a similar experience as in the Stories for People analytics with minor changes.

Some changes include cancel, preview query, finish buttons removed from the bottom. Preview button is moved to the top of tool bar.

  • Once the model is complete. You can save it in a folder to be used by Stories.

Note:  The Model saved as a public model in any accessible folder. This means you can now re-use the same model in multiple stories.

You can now design a story using this model.

Pro-Tip: you can design stories with highly aggregated widgets delivered via acquired connection, with a linked analysis to a table widget that is using the live connection to display employee level data.
This can be a important design consideration to use coarse grain Data Access Control in Datasphere, leverage the live connection and the fine-grained Role based Permission to control what a user can see on employee level.

 

Restrictions to SAP SuccessFactors Live Data Connections
  • Classic Design Experience
  • Scheduled Publication (Planned)
  • Data Change Insights
  • Model Repointing
  • Time Hierarchies
  • R Widgets
  • Search To Insights
  • Geo / Map Functionality
  • Natural Language Queries

All the restrictions applicable to SAP HANA live connections are also valid here.
